summary: Bosnia and Herzegovina is a fragile post-war state held together by the 1995 Dayton Accords, divided between a Bosniak-Croat federation and a Serb republic, with the EU and NATO still overseeing stability.

history.detail: Bosnia becomes Ottoman; many convert to Islam, shaping its mixed identity.
The empire occupies and later annexes Bosnia.
Bosnia secedes from Yugoslavia; war and siege of Sarajevo begin.
Serb forces murder ~8,000 Bosniak men and boys — Europe's worst post-WWII atrocity.
US-brokered peace splits the country into two entities.
Granted amid renewed Serb secession threats.

finance.currency_role: Convertible mark (BAM); pegged to the euro
finance.central_bank: Central Bank of Bosnia and Herzegovina (currency board)

follow_the_money.detail: Money from Bosnians abroad is a major share of household income
Real estate and tourism funding, especially around Sarajevo

theories_disputed.detail: ICTY and ICJ ruled it genocide; some Republika Srpska leaders still deny it.
Serb entity leader Dodik repeatedly threatens to split away, testing Dayton.

connections.detail: Backs the Bosnian Serb entity; war legacy unresolved
Investment, cultural and religious ties with Bosniaks
